title: Fiber Identification Decision Tree
description: Apply a multi-step forensic protocol (burn test, microscopy, solubility) to classify an unknown textile fiber. Read the observed test results for a sample, then answer Yes/No at each decision node to walk a branching tree to a classification (Cotton, Wool, Silk, Nylon, or Polyester).
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
Trace Evidence
Fiber Analysis
Microscopy
Burn Test
bloomLevel: Apply (L3) — apply a multi-step identification protocol to classify an unknown fiber.
chapter_title: Hair, Fiber, and Trace Evidence Analysis

educational.topic: Classifying textile fibers with a branching burn / microscopy / solubility protocol
learningObjectives: Apply a sequential identification protocol (burn test, then microscopy, then solubility) to an unknown fiber.
Use observed test results to choose Yes/No at each decision node and reach a classification.
Distinguish natural (cellulosic and protein) fibers from synthetic fibers by their burn behavior and residue.
educational.prerequisites: Familiarity with reading a flowchart; the idea that fibers can be natural or synthetic

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Top-down branching decision tree with diamond decision nodes and leaf classifications. A sample card lists the observed burn, microscopy, and solubility results; the student answers Yes/No to traverse the highlighted node and gets per-step match feedback, ending at a leaf marked correct or incorrect.
pedagogical.assessment: Students read the sample's observed results, answer each decision correctly, and reach the matching fiber leaf (green check); diverging answers are flagged so the student sees where the logic broke down
pedagogical.modelType: Deterministic decision tree: each node tests one boolean property of the randomly assigned sample (melts, burning-hair smell, hard black bead, bleach-soluble) that the student infers from the observation card
controls: Start New Sample button (assigns a random unknown fiber with preset test results)
